 {"id":"bd-2pb6e","title":"Task: Implement mode-to-agent assignment strategies in internal/ensemble/assignment.go","description":"# Task: Implement Mode-to-Agent Assignment Strategies

## Location
internal/ensemble/assignment.go

## Purpose
Assign reasoning modes to agent panes while respecting agent strengths, user preferences, and available pane types.

## Assignment Strategies

### 1. Round-Robin (default)
Distribute modes evenly across available panes.
```go
func AssignRoundRobin(modes []string, panes []tmux.Pane) []ModeAssignment
```

### 2. Category-Affinity (intelligent)
Prefer agent types based on **ModeCategory** (A–L taxonomy), not hard-coded mode IDs.
```go
// Category -> preferred agent types (ordered)
var CategoryAffinities = map[ModeCategory][]string{
    CategoryFormal:      {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryUncertainty: {"cod", "cc"},
    CategoryCausal:      {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryPractical:   {"cod", "cc"},
    CategoryStrategic:   {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryDialectical: {"cc"},
    CategoryModal:       {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryDomain:      {"cc", "gmi"},
    CategoryMeta:        {"cc"},
    CategoryAmpliative:  {"gmi", "cc"},
    CategoryVagueness:   {"gmi", "cc"},
    CategoryChange:      {"cc", "cod"},
}

func AssignByCategory(modes []string, panes []tmux.Pane, catalog *ModeCatalog) []ModeAssignment
```
- Resolve mode by **id or code**
- Pick available pane of preferred type; fallback to any

### 3. Explicit (user-specified)
User provides exact mapping: `abductive:cc,adversarial:cod` or `A1:cc`.
```go
func AssignExplicit(specs []string, panes []tmux.Pane) ([]ModeAssignment, error)
```

## Helper Functions
```go
func groupPanesByType(panes []tmux.Pane) map[string][]tmux.Pane
func pickAvailablePane(byType map[string][]tmux.Pane, preferred []string, used []ModeAssignment) tmux.Pane
func ValidateAssignments(assignments []ModeAssignment, modes []string) error
```

## Determinism + Logging
- Assignment order must be deterministic (stable ordering by mode id/code).
- Tie-breaking is stable and logged (preferred type chosen + fallback reason).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Round-robin distributes evenly
- [ ] Category-affinity uses taxonomy categories (A–L) only
- [ ] Explicit assignment parses id or code
- [ ] Handles more modes than panes (error)
- [ ] Handles more panes than modes (some panes unused)
- [ ] Validation catches invalid configurations
- [ ] Logs assignment decisions + fallbacks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:36:16.389957944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:43:51.069750128Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T22:43:51.069729149Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pb6e","depends_on_id":"bd-2qfa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2pgo6","title":"CLI: Add tier threshold override flags","description":"## Purpose
Implement --prompt flag to provide initial marching orders that will be injected into all spawned agents after launch.

## Implementation Details

```go
// Flag definition
var initialPrompt string
var promptFile   string

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&initialPrompt, "prompt", "",
        "Initial prompt to inject into all agents")
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&promptFile, "prompt-file", "",
        "File containing initial prompt (mutually exclusive with --prompt)")
}

// Validation
func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    logger := slog.Default()
    
    // Mutual exclusivity check
    if initialPrompt != "" && promptFile != "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("--prompt and --prompt-file are mutually exclusive")
    }
    
    // Load prompt from file if specified
    if promptFile != "" {
        data, err := os.ReadFile(promptFile)
        if err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("read prompt file %q: %w", promptFile, err)
        }
        initialPrompt = string(data)
        logger.Info("loaded prompt from file",
            "path", promptFile,
            "length", len(initialPrompt))
    }
    
    if initialPrompt != "" {
        logger.Info("initial prompt configured",
            "length", len(initialPrompt),
            "preview", truncate(initialPrompt, 50))
    } else {
        logger.Debug("no initial prompt configured")
    }
    
    // Store in plan for injection phase
    plan.InitialPrompt = initialPrompt
    
    // ... continue ...
}

// After agent launch, inject prompt
func injectInitialPrompts(plan *swarm.SwarmPlan) error {
    if plan.InitialPrompt == "" {
        return nil
    }
    
    injector := swarm.NewPromptInjector(logger)
    for _, session := range plan.Sessions {
        for _, pane := range session.Panes {
            if err := injector.Inject(pane, plan.InitialPrompt); err != nil {
                logger.Error("failed to inject prompt",
                    "pane", pane.Index,
                    "error", err)
            }
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log prompt length (not full content for large prompts)
- Log first 50 chars as preview
- Log prompt source (flag vs file)
- Log each injection attempt and result

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --prompt for inline text
- [ ] --prompt-file for file input
- [ ] Mutual exclusivity enforced
- [ ] File read error handling
- [ ] Preview logging (truncated)
- [ ] Injection after all agents launched
- [ ] Empty prompt means no injection","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonEagle","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:22.621959652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T20:53:58.962917062Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pgo6","depends_on_id":"bd-3ixg4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pgo6","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2pl9v","title":"Task: Write preamble content for all modes (core files + advanced fallback)","description":"# Task: Write preamble content for all modes (core files + advanced fallback)

Each mode preamble explains the reasoning lens without exposing implementation details to users.

## Strategy
- **Core tier (~28):** hand-authored preamble files.
- **Advanced tier (remaining modes):** auto-generated fallback preamble derived from mode metadata (description, outputs, best_for, failure_modes). This ensures **all 80 modes are usable** without requiring bespoke files.

## Preamble File Format (Core Modes)
```yaml
# internal/ensemble/preambles/deductive.yaml
id: deductive
code: A1
name: Deductive Inference
tier: core
preamble: |
  ## Your Reasoning Mode: Deductive Inference
  ...
```

## File Location
internal/ensemble/preambles/*.yaml

## Fallback Behavior (Advanced Modes)
- If no file exists for a mode, PreambleEngine renders:
  - Mode name + description
  - Outputs, best_for, failure_modes
  - Differentiator (if present)
  - Tier warning ("advanced" mode)

## Validation / Tests
- Preambles must pass bd-xkrnc quality validation (required sections, schema contract fields, token budget)
- No internal implementation details or private instructions in preamble text

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All **core** modes have preamble files
- [ ] **All 80 modes** can render a preamble (file or fallback)
- [ ] Preamble includes id, code, name, tier
- [ ] Content matches `modes_of_reasoning.md` for core modes
- [ ] Fallback preamble uses mode metadata deterministically","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:33:36.480494155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:45:09.268301537Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:45:09.268260931Z","close_reason":"Added core preamble YAML files for all TierCore modes; fallback remains metadata-driven.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-1hlhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2plo3","title":"Session health dashboard should show OAuth/rate-limit status per agent","description":"Session health dashboard should show OAuth/rate-limit status per agent.

## Problem Being Solved

When running multi-agent swarms, agents frequently hit rate limits or OAuth issues. Currently there's no visibility into which agents are affected and why.

## Requirements

### Dashboard Widget
Add a new widget to the session health dashboard showing:
1. Per-agent OAuth status (valid/expired/error)
2. Per-agent rate limit status (ok/warning/limited)
3. Last API call timestamp
4. Error count in last N minutes

### Data Sources
- OAuth status: detect from agent output patterns
- Rate limit status: integrate with ratelimit.Tracker
- Timestamps: from pane capture output

### Visual Design
```
OAuth/Rate Status:
  cc-1: OAuth=✓ Rate=OK   Last=2m ago
  cc-2: OAuth=✓ Rate=WARN Last=30s ago (3 limits in 5m)
  cod-1: OAuth=✓ Rate=OK  Last=1m ago
  gmi-1: OAuth=\! Rate=LIMITED Last=5m ago (needs reauth)
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-health-oauth=SESSION
Returns: JSON with per-agent OAuth and rate limit status

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Dashboard widget shows OAuth status per agent (valid/expired/error)
2. Dashboard widget shows rate limit status per agent (ok/warning/limited)
3. Warning status triggered at 3+ rate limits in 5 minutes
4. Limited status triggered when agent reports rate limit error
5. Robot mode command returns machine-readable status
6. Status updates within 30 seconds of state change

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/dashboard/oauth_widget_test.go
- Test status detection from output patterns
- Test warning/limited threshold logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-OAUTH-DASH] OAuth Dashboard Widget Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-OAUTH-DASH]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing OAuth dashboard widget"
TEST_SESSION="e2e_oauth_$$"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=1 --no-prompt
sleep 5
# Check robot health command exists
ntm --robot-health-oauth="$TEST_SESSION" | jq -e '.agents' || echo "WARN: command not yet implemented"
ntm kill "$TEST_SESSION" --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX OAuth dashboard test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session health dashboard shows OAuth status per agent
2. Rate limit status visible for each provider
3. Color coding for healthy/warning/critical
4. Real-time updates from rate limit tracker
5. Unit tests for status rendering
6. E2E test verifying dashboard accuracy","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-20T05:28:28.060979675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:36:03.259569379Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-2plo3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: Implemented --robot-health-oauth flag with OAuth/rate-limit status detection and RateLimitPanel dashboard panel. Tests pass. Remaining: Wire panel into TUI layout, add E2E test.","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:36:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2prx","title":"Task: Implement GenerateHandoff() function","description":"## Purpose
Create the core handoff generation function that produces structured context for session transitions.

## Scope
- Implement GenerateHandoff() in internal/handoff/generator.go
- Gather current session state (active files, recent changes, pending tasks)
- Query BV for in-progress beads assigned to this agent
- Query Agent Mail for active threads and file reservations
- Format into structured handoff document

## Acceptance Criteria
- GenerateHandoff() returns complete HandoffContext struct
- Includes all active beads with status
- Includes pending file reservations with expiry times
- Includes recent Agent Mail threads
- Includes uncommitted git changes summary
- Output is JSON-serializable for storage

## Technical Notes
- Use existing agentmail client from internal/agentmail/
- Integrate with bv client for bead queries
- Consider including token usage metrics for context budget guidance","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:59:08.323052801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:08:31.693991971Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:08:31.693947347Z","close_reason":"Implemented GenerateHandoff() with BV and Agent Mail integration. Added options struct, graceful degradation, status inference, JSON serialization, and comprehensive tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2prx","depends_on_id":"bd-2i44","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
